universe.eligibility_scope: MSCI Global Investable Market Indexes (GIMI) constituents; Standard size-segment in all Developed and Emerging Markets, and Small Cap size-segment in the USA

details.condition: Companies not assessed by MSCI Solutions on data for any of these products are not eligible for inclusion

new_rules.name: require_esg_rating_bb_or_above
new_rules.purpose: Restrict the index to companies that have demonstrated an ability to manage ESG risks and opportunities by requiring a minimum MSCI ESG Rating of BB; applies to both new candidates and existing constituents at each Annual Index Review.

source.markdown: Companies are required to have an MSCI ESG Rating of 'BB' or above to be eligible for inclusion in the Indexes.
source.section: 2.2.1 ESG Ratings Eligibility

technical: no null values in msci_esg_rating after filter
business: all remaining rows have msci_esg_rating in {AAA, AA, A, BBB, BB}; no row with msci_esg_rating in {B, CCC} survives

new_rules.name: require_controversies_score_3_or_above
new_rules.purpose: Exclude new index candidates that are involved in very serious controversies by requiring an MSCI Controversies Score of 3 or above; applies to securities not currently in the index.

source.markdown: Companies are required to have an MSCI Controversies Score of 3 or above to be eligible for inclusion in the Indexes.
source.section: 2.2.2 Controversies Score Eligibility

business: all non-constituent rows have msci_controversies_score >= 3; no non-constituent with score < 3 survives

new_rules.name: retain_existing_if_controversies_score_1_or_above
new_rules.purpose: Maintain existing index constituents in the Eligible Universe at each Annual Index Review if their Controversies Score is 1 or above, providing a buffer relative to the stricter threshold applied to new additions.

source.markdown: Existing constituents of the Indexes are maintained in the Eligible Universe if they meet all the following conditions: ... MSCI Controversies Score of 1 or above
source.section: 3.1.1 Updating the Eligible Universe

business: all existing-constituent rows surviving have msci_controversies_score >= 1; no existing constituent with score = 0 survives

new_rules.name: exclude_civilian_firearms_producer
new_rules.purpose: Exclude companies classified as 'Producer' of firearms and small arms ammunition for civilian markets, under the Highly Restrictive business involvement screen. Producer classification triggers exclusion regardless of revenue share; companies catering exclusively to military, government, and law enforcement markets are not in scope.

source.markdown: All companies classified as "Producer" of firearms and small arms ammunitions for civilian markets. It does not include companies that cater to the military, government, and law enforcement markets.
source.section: Appendix II: Controversial Business Involvement Criteria

business: no surviving row has civilian_firearms_producer_flag = True

new_rules.name: exclude_civilian_firearms_revenue_above_5pct
new_rules.purpose: Exclude companies deriving 5% or more aggregate revenue from the production and distribution (wholesale or retail) of firearms or small arms ammunition intended for civilian use, under the Highly Restrictive business involvement screen.

source.markdown: All companies deriving 5% or more aggregate revenue from the production and distribution (wholesale or retail) of firearms or small arms ammunition intended for civilian use
source.section: Appendix II: Controversial Business Involvement Criteria

business: no surviving row has civilian_firearms_revenue_pct >= 0.05

new_rules.name: exclude_tobacco_producer
new_rules.purpose: Exclude companies that manufacture tobacco products (cigars, cigarettes, e-cigarettes, smokeless tobacco, etc.) or grow or process raw tobacco leaves, under the Highly Restrictive business involvement screen.

source.markdown: All companies that manufacture tobacco products, such as cigars, blunts, cigarettes, e-cigarettes, inhalers, beedis, kreteks, smokeless tobacco, snuff, snus, dissolvable and chewing tobacco. This also includes companies that grow or process raw tobacco leaves.
source.section: Appendix II: Controversial Business Involvement Criteria

business: no surviving row has tobacco_producer_flag = True

new_rules.name: exclude_tobacco_revenue_above_5pct
new_rules.purpose: Exclude companies deriving 5% or more aggregate revenue from the production, distribution, retail, supply, and licensing of tobacco-related products, under the Highly Restrictive business involvement screen.

source.markdown: All companies deriving 5% or more aggregate revenue from the production, distribution, retail, supply and licensing of tobacco-related products
source.section: Appendix II: Controversial Business Involvement Criteria

business: no surviving row has tobacco_revenue_pct >= 0.05

new_rules.name: exclude_alcohol_above_10pct
new_rules.purpose: Exclude companies deriving 10% or more revenue from the production of alcohol-related products, under the Least Restrictive business involvement screen.

source.markdown: All companies deriving 10% or more revenue from the production of alcohol-related products
source.section: Appendix II: Controversial Business Involvement Criteria

business: no surviving row has alcohol_revenue_pct >= 0.10

new_rules.name: exclude_conventional_weapons_above_10pct
new_rules.purpose: Exclude companies deriving 10% or more revenue from the production of conventional weapons and components, under the Least Restrictive business involvement screen.

source.markdown: All companies deriving 10% or more revenue from the production of conventional weapons and components
source.section: Appendix II: Controversial Business Involvement Criteria

business: no surviving row has conventional_weapons_revenue_pct >= 0.10

new_rules.name: exclude_gambling_above_10pct
new_rules.purpose: Exclude companies deriving 10% or more revenue from ownership or operation of gambling-related business activities, under the Least Restrictive business involvement screen.

source.markdown: All companies deriving 10% or more revenue from ownership of operation of gambling-related business activities
source.section: Appendix II: Controversial Business Involvement Criteria

business: no surviving row has gambling_revenue_pct >= 0.10

new_rules.name: exclude_nuclear_power_above_10pct
new_rules.purpose: Exclude companies deriving 10% or more revenue from the ownership or operation of nuclear power plants, under the Least Restrictive business involvement screen.

source.markdown: All companies deriving 10% or more revenue from the ownership or operation of nuclear power plants
source.section: Appendix II: Controversial Business Involvement Criteria

business: no surviving row has nuclear_power_revenue_pct >= 0.10

new_rules.name: exclude_fossil_fuel_extraction_above_5pct
new_rules.purpose: Exclude companies deriving 5% or more aggregate revenue (reported or estimated) from thermal coal mining and unconventional oil and gas extraction, as a combined Fossil Fuel Extraction screen with no assigned restrictiveness level.

source.markdown: All companies deriving 5% or more aggregate revenue (either reported or estimated) from thermal coal mining and unconventional oil and gas extraction.
source.section: Appendix II: Controversial Business Involvement Criteria

technical: thermal_coal_mining_revenue_pct and unconventional_oil_gas_revenue_pct columns present after filter
business: no surviving row has (thermal_coal_mining_revenue_pct + unconventional_oil_gas_revenue_pct) >= 0.05

new_rules.name: exclude_thermal_coal_power_above_5pct
new_rules.purpose: Exclude companies deriving 5% or more revenue (reported or estimated) from thermal coal-based power generation, as a screen with no assigned restrictiveness level.

source.markdown: All companies deriving 5% or more revenue (either reported or estimated) from thermal coal-based power generation.
source.section: Appendix II: Controversial Business Involvement Criteria

business: no surviving row has thermal_coal_power_revenue_pct >= 0.05

new_rules.name: exclude_palm_oil_above_5pct
new_rules.purpose: Exclude companies deriving 5% or more revenue from the production of Palm Oil.

source.markdown: All companies deriving 5% or more revenue from the production of Palm Oil
source.section: Appendix II: Controversial Business Involvement Criteria

business: no surviving row has palm_oil_revenue_pct >= 0.05

new_rules.name: exclude_arctic_oil_gas_above_5pct
new_rules.purpose: Exclude companies deriving 5% or more aggregate revenue from arctic oil and arctic gas production north of 66.5 degrees latitude, covering offshore and onshore production.

source.markdown: All companies deriving 5% or more aggregate revenue from arctic oil and arctic gas production. The definition of Arctic is geographical and includes production activities north of the 66.5 latitude.
source.section: Appendix II: Controversial Business Involvement Criteria

business: no surviving row has arctic_oil_gas_revenue_pct >= 0.05

new_rules.name: rank_by_esg_rating_membership_industry_score_ff_mcap
new_rules.purpose: Rank eligible securities within each GICS sector by ESG Rating (highest first), then by current Selection Index membership (existing constituents above non-constituents), then by industry-adjusted ESG score (highest first), then by free float-adjusted market capitalization (largest first), to achieve stable, ESG-quality-led ordering before coverage selection.

source.markdown: For each sector, eligible securities of the regional Parent Index as described in Section 2.2 are ranked based on the following criteria: ESG Rating; Current Index membership (existing constituents above non-constituents of the Indexes); Industry-adjusted ESG scores; Decreasing free float-adjusted market capitalization
source.section: 3.1.2 Ranking of Eligible Securities

technical: rank column added; no null ranks; rank is unique within (gics_sector, security_id)
business: within a sector, any row with a higher ESG rating has a strictly lower rank number than any row with a lower ESG rating
within the same ESG rating, current constituent rows have lower rank numbers than non-constituent rows
within the same ESG rating and membership status, higher industry_adjusted_esg_score implies lower rank number

new_rules.name: select_by_sector_coverage_tiers
new_rules.purpose: Select securities per sector from the ranked Eligible Universe using four sequential tiers until 50% cumulative free float-adjusted market capitalization coverage is reached; apply marginal-company logic and a 45% floor guarantee per Appendix I.

source.markdown: For each sector, eligible securities of the regional Parent Index are then selected from the ranked Eligible Universe in the following order until the target 50% coverage by cumulative free float-adjusted market capitalization is reached: Securities in the top 35% ... 'AAA' rated securities in the top 50% ... Current Index constituents in the top 65% ... Remaining eligible securities
source.section: 3.1.3 Selection of Eligible Securities

technical: selected column added as boolean; no null values
only rows with selected=True appear in downstream pipeline
business: cumulative ff_market_cap coverage per gics_sector is >= 0.45 whenever at least one eligible security exists in that sector
no ineligible security (per section 3.1.1) is selected even if sector coverage remains below 0.50
if the marginal company is a current constituent, it is always included
if the marginal company is not a current constituent, it is included only if coverage with it is closer to 0.50 than coverage without it

new_rules.name: add_new_parent_addition_if_eligible_and_sector_below_45pct
new_rules.purpose: Add a security newly included in the Parent Index (IPO, early inclusion, size-segment migration) to the Selection Index on the date of its Parent Index inclusion if it meets the Section 2.2 eligibility criteria and the cumulative ff-mcap coverage of its sector is currently below 45%.

source.markdown: New securities added to the Parent Index (such as IPOs, other early inclusions and migrations from a different size-segment) will be added to the Indexes on the date of security inclusion only if they meet the eligibility criteria described in Section 2.2 and the market capitalization coverage of the sector to which the security belongs is less than 45%.
source.section: 3.3 Ongoing Event-Related Maintenance

business: no security is added between reviews to a sector where current coverage >= 0.45
no ineligible security is added even if sector coverage < 0.45

new_rules.name: delete_quarterly_if_fails_annual_eligibility
new_rules.purpose: Delete existing Selection Index constituents at each Quarterly Index Review (February, August, November) if they no longer meet the eligibility criteria defined in Section 3.1.1 (ESG rating, Controversies Score, CBI screens).

source.markdown: At the Quarterly Index Reviews, existing constituents are deleted from the Indexes if they do not meet the eligibility criteria described in Section 3.1.1. Existing constituents that meet the eligibility criteria are retained in the index.
source.section: 3.2 Quarterly Index Reviews

business: no surviving constituent has msci_esg_rating below BB after quarterly deletion
no surviving constituent has msci_controversies_score < 1 after quarterly deletion
no surviving constituent has cbi_screen_flag = True after quarterly deletion

new_rules.name: add_quarterly_if_eligible_and_sector_below_45pct
new_rules.purpose: Make additions at each Quarterly Index Review only to sectors where cumulative ff-mcap coverage has fallen below 45% (using a 10% buffer on the 50% target), selecting from eligible securities per Section 2.2 until 50% coverage is restored.

source.markdown: Additions, from the eligible securities as per Section 2.2, are made only to those sectors where the current market capitalization coverage is less than 45%, until the 50% target is reached. Market price movements may cause small deviations in the sector coverage between two Index Reviews. Therefore, in order to minimize turnover, a buffer of 10% is used on the target coverage of 50% to define under-representation.
source.section: 3.2 Quarterly Index Reviews

business: no additions are made to a sector with current coverage >= 0.45
no ineligible security is added at quarterly review

reused_rules.name: exclude_controversial_weapons_involvement
reused_rules.justification: Most Restrictive screen: all companies with any tie to Controversial Weapons (cluster munitions, landmines, depleted uranium, biological/chemical, blinding lasers, non-detectable fragments, incendiary weapons) as defined by the XCW methodology. Appendix II cites the XCW methodology URL directly.

reused_rules.name: exclude_nuclear_weapons
reused_rules.justification: Highly Restrictive screen covering manufacturers of warheads, missiles, components developed for exclusive nuclear use, delivery platforms, auxiliary service providers, and manufacturers of dual-use components. Appendix II lists the same criteria.

reused_rules.name: spinoff_deferred_to_review
reused_rules.justification: Section 3.3 states all spin-off securities from an existing constituent will not be added at event implementation; reevaluation occurs at the subsequent Index Review. Section 3.3 explicitly defers further detail to the MSCI Corporate Events Methodology book.

reused_rules.name: ma_delete_acquired_constituent
reused_rules.justification: Section 3.3 states if an existing constituent is acquired by a non-constituent, the constituent is deleted from the index. Standard corporate-events treatment; Section 3.3 defers further detail to the MSCI Corporate Events Methodology book.

reused_rules.name: ma_no_add_nonconstituent_acquirer
reused_rules.justification: Section 3.3 states the acquiring non-constituent will not be added to the index following an M&A event. Section 3.3 defers further detail to the MSCI Corporate Events Methodology book.

reused_rules.name: characteristics_change_no_deletion
reused_rules.justification: Section 3.3 states a security continues as a constituent if there are changes in characteristics (country, sector, size segment); reevaluation occurs at subsequent Index Review. Section 3.3 defers further detail to the MSCI Corporate Events Methodology book.

reused_rules.name: parent_deletion_propagates
reused_rules.justification: Section 3.3 states Parent Index deletions will be reflected simultaneously in the Selection Indexes. Section 3.3 defers further detail to the MSCI Corporate Events Methodology book.

content.tier1: All securities in top 35% cumulative ff-mcap of ranked universe (including security that pushes coverage above 35%)
content.tier2: AAA-rated securities in top 50% cumulative ff-mcap of ranked universe (including security that pushes coverage above 50%)
content.tier3: Current index constituents in top 65% cumulative ff-mcap of ranked universe (including security that pushes coverage above 65% if that security is a current constituent)
content.tier4: Remaining eligible securities in ranked universe
content.marginal_company_logic: If marginal company is a current constituent, always selected. If not a current constituent, selected only if coverage with it is closer to 50% than coverage without it. Always selected if required to achieve 45% floor.
content.constructed_at: regional level for Standard size-segment; at USA level for Small Cap

intra_review_invariants.quote: No new securities will be added (except where noted below) to the Index between Index Reviews. For cases where additions are noted below, securities will be added to the index only if added to the Parent Index.
intra_review_invariants.concern: General no-add policy between reviews; the only intra-review additions are those explicitly enumerated in the corporate-events table (new Parent Index additions subject to the section-2.2 + sector-coverage<45% gates). Spin-offs and M&A acquirers are explicitly excluded from intra-review addition.

intra_review_invariants.quote: There are no deletions from the Indexes between Index Reviews on account of a security becoming ineligible because of MSCI ESG Rating downgrade and/or decrease in MSCI Controversies Score and/or change in business involvement.
intra_review_invariants.concern: Negative ESG or CBI changes between reviews do not trigger mid-cycle deletion; only quarterly and annual reviews can remove a security on ESG/CBI grounds.

intra_review_invariants.quote: Parent Index deletions will be reflected simultaneously.
intra_review_invariants.concern: Parent Index deletions are the one class of intra-review removal that does propagate immediately, independent of ESG/CBI status.

schedules.name: Annual Index Review
schedules.data_cutoff: end of April (month preceding the Index Review); late-published data accepted when available after month-end
schedules.announcement_lead: nine business days before effective date

schedules.name: Quarterly Index Review
schedules.data_cutoff: end of January, July, and October respectively; late-published data accepted when available after month-end
schedules.announcement_lead: nine business days before effective date

mandatory_fields.name: msci_esg_rating
mandatory_fields.values: AAA AA A BBB BB B CCC

preprocessing.step: Use end-of-preceding-month ESG data; substitute later-published data for securities where month-end data is unavailable

mock_datapoints.name: msci_esg_rating
mock_datapoints.description: MSCI ESG Rating on a seven-point scale from AAA (best) to CCC (worst), reflecting company management of ESG risks and opportunities relative to industry peers.
definition_notes: Derived from industry-adjusted ESG score (0-10) via a mapping to the letter scale
Industry-normalized: same numeric score may yield different letter rating across sectors
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: 'BB' or above to be eligible for inclusion in the Indexes
mock_datapoints.source_section: 2.2.1 ESG Ratings Eligibility
mock_datapoints.taxonomy: MSCI ESG Rating seven-point letter scale (AAA, AA, A, BBB, BB, B, CCC)

mock_datapoints.name: msci_controversies_score
mock_datapoints.description: MSCI Controversies Score on a 0-10 scale; 0 represents the most severe controversy, aligned with UN Declaration of Human Rights, ILO Fundamental Principles, and UN Global Compact.
definition_notes: MSCI Controversies Score falls on a 0-10 scale, with '0' being the most severe controversy
Used at two thresholds in this methodology: 3 or above for new additions, 1 or above for existing constituents at Annual Review
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: MSCI Controversies Score of 3 or above to be eligible for inclusion in the Indexes; MSCI Controversies Score of 1 or above for existing constituents
mock_datapoints.source_section: 2.2.2 Controversies Score Eligibility
mock_datapoints.taxonomy: MSCI Controversies Score 0-10 scale (0 = most severe)

mock_datapoints.name: industry_adjusted_esg_score
mock_datapoints.description: Numeric ESG score (0-10) normalized by industry, used as tiebreaker in sector ranking after ESG Rating and index membership.
definition_notes: Produced by MSCI ESG Ratings methodology via weighted average key issue calculation, normalized by industry
Translated to the seven-point AAA-CCC letter scale
mock_datapoints.source_section: 4.1 MSCI ESG Ratings

mock_datapoints.name: ff_market_cap
mock_datapoints.description: Free float-adjusted market capitalization in USD; used in sector coverage calculation, ranking tiebreaker, and as the basis for index weighting.
definition_notes: Used to compute cumulative sector coverage relative to the Parent Index
Basis of index weighting (free float-adjusted market capitalization-weighted)
mock_datapoints.source_section: 2.3 Index Construction

mock_datapoints.name: gics_sector
mock_datapoints.distribution: choices(['Energy','Materials','Industrials','Consumer Discretionary','Consumer Staples','Health Care','Financials','Information Technology','Communication Services','Utilities','Real Estate'])
mock_datapoints.description: GICS sector classification used to group securities for sector-level coverage targeting (50% of Parent Index ff-mcap per sector).
mock_datapoints.source_section: 2.3 Index Construction

mock_datapoints.name: is_current_constituent
mock_datapoints.description: Flag indicating whether the security is currently an index constituent; existing constituents receive priority in ranking and benefit from the relaxed Controversies Score threshold (>=1 vs. >=3) at Annual Review.
mock_datapoints.source_section: 3.1.1 Updating the Eligible Universe

mock_datapoints.name: civilian_firearms_producer_flag
mock_datapoints.description: Flag for companies classified as 'Producer' of firearms and small arms ammunition for civilian markets (excludes companies catering exclusively to military, government, and law enforcement markets).
definition_notes: It does not include companies that cater to the military, government, and law enforcement markets
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: All companies classified as "Producer" of firearms and small arms ammunitions for civilian markets
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix II: Controversial Business Involvement Criteria
mock_datapoints.taxonomy: MSCI CBI restrictiveness level: Highly Restrictive

mock_datapoints.name: civilian_firearms_revenue_pct
mock_datapoints.description: Aggregate revenue percentage from production and distribution (wholesale or retail) of firearms or small arms ammunition intended for civilian use.
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: 5% or more aggregate revenue from the production and distribution (wholesale or retail) of firearms or small arms ammunition intended for civilian use
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix II: Controversial Business Involvement Criteria
mock_datapoints.taxonomy: MSCI CBI restrictiveness level: Highly Restrictive

mock_datapoints.name: tobacco_producer_flag
mock_datapoints.description: Flag for companies that manufacture tobacco products (cigars, cigarettes, e-cigarettes, smokeless tobacco, etc.) or grow or process raw tobacco leaves.
definition_notes: Manufactured products covered: cigars, blunts, cigarettes, e-cigarettes, inhalers, beedis, kreteks, smokeless tobacco, snuff, snus, dissolvable and chewing tobacco
Also includes companies that grow or process raw tobacco leaves
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: All companies that manufacture tobacco products ... This also includes companies that grow or process raw tobacco leaves
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix II: Controversial Business Involvement Criteria
mock_datapoints.taxonomy: MSCI CBI restrictiveness level: Highly Restrictive

mock_datapoints.name: tobacco_revenue_pct
mock_datapoints.description: Aggregate revenue percentage from production, distribution, retail, supply, and licensing of tobacco-related products.
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: 5% or more aggregate revenue from the production, distribution, retail, supply and licensing of tobacco-related products
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix II: Controversial Business Involvement Criteria
mock_datapoints.taxonomy: MSCI CBI restrictiveness level: Highly Restrictive

mock_datapoints.name: alcohol_revenue_pct
mock_datapoints.description: Revenue percentage from the production of alcohol-related products.
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: 10% or more revenue from the production of alcohol-related products
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix II: Controversial Business Involvement Criteria
mock_datapoints.taxonomy: MSCI CBI restrictiveness level: Least Restrictive

mock_datapoints.name: conventional_weapons_revenue_pct
mock_datapoints.description: Revenue percentage from the production of conventional weapons and components.
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: 10% or more revenue from the production of conventional weapons and components
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix II: Controversial Business Involvement Criteria
mock_datapoints.taxonomy: MSCI CBI restrictiveness level: Least Restrictive

mock_datapoints.name: gambling_revenue_pct
mock_datapoints.description: Revenue percentage from ownership or operation of gambling-related business activities.
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: 10% or more revenue from ownership of operation of gambling-related business activities
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix II: Controversial Business Involvement Criteria
mock_datapoints.taxonomy: MSCI CBI restrictiveness level: Least Restrictive

mock_datapoints.name: nuclear_power_revenue_pct
mock_datapoints.description: Revenue percentage from ownership or operation of nuclear power plants.
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: 10% or more revenue from the ownership or operation of nuclear power plants
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix II: Controversial Business Involvement Criteria
mock_datapoints.taxonomy: MSCI CBI restrictiveness level: Least Restrictive

mock_datapoints.name: thermal_coal_mining_revenue_pct
mock_datapoints.description: Revenue from the mining of thermal coal (lignite, bituminous, anthracite, steam coal) and its sale to external parties.
definition_notes: Does not cover revenue from metallurgical coal
Does not cover coal mined for internal power generation (e.g., vertically integrated power producers)
Does not cover intra-company sales of mined thermal coal
Does not cover revenue from coal trading
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: Combined with unconventional oil & gas extraction: 5% or more aggregate revenue (either reported or estimated) from thermal coal mining and unconventional oil and gas extraction
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix II: Controversial Business Involvement Criteria
mock_datapoints.taxonomy: MSCI CBI: Fossil Fuel Extraction (no specific restrictiveness level applied)

mock_datapoints.name: unconventional_oil_gas_revenue_pct
mock_datapoints.description: Revenue from oil sands, oil shale (kerogen-rich deposits), shale gas, shale oil, coal seam gas, and coal bed methane.
definition_notes: Does not cover all types of conventional oil and gas production including Arctic onshore/offshore, deep water, shallow water and other onshore/offshore
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: Combined with thermal coal mining: 5% or more aggregate revenue (either reported or estimated) from thermal coal mining and unconventional oil and gas extraction
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix II: Controversial Business Involvement Criteria
mock_datapoints.taxonomy: MSCI CBI: Fossil Fuel Extraction (no specific restrictiveness level applied)

mock_datapoints.name: thermal_coal_power_revenue_pct
mock_datapoints.description: Revenue (reported or estimated) from thermal coal-based power generation.
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: 5% or more revenue (either reported or estimated) from thermal coal-based power generation
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix II: Controversial Business Involvement Criteria
mock_datapoints.taxonomy: MSCI CBI: Thermal Coal Power (no specific restrictiveness level applied)

mock_datapoints.name: palm_oil_revenue_pct
mock_datapoints.description: Revenue from the production of Palm Oil.
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: 5% or more revenue from the production of Palm Oil
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix II: Controversial Business Involvement Criteria
mock_datapoints.taxonomy: MSCI CBI: Palm Oil (no specific restrictiveness level applied)

mock_datapoints.name: arctic_oil_gas_revenue_pct
mock_datapoints.description: Aggregate revenue from arctic oil and arctic gas production north of 66.5 degrees latitude, including offshore and onshore production.
definition_notes: The definition of Arctic is geographical and includes production activities north of the 66.5 latitude
Includes offshore or onshore oil and gas production
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: 5% or more aggregate revenue from arctic oil and arctic gas production
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix II: Controversial Business Involvement Criteria
mock_datapoints.taxonomy: MSCI CBI: Arctic Oil & Gas (no specific restrictiveness level applied)

mock_datapoints.name: sector_current_ff_mcap_coverage
mock_datapoints.description: Current cumulative free float-adjusted market capitalization coverage of the GICS sector relative to the Parent Index sector; used to determine whether additions are permitted between reviews and at quarterly review.
mock_datapoints.source_section: 3.2 Quarterly Index Reviews

mock_datapoints.name: is_eligible_section_2_2
mock_datapoints.description: Composite eligibility flag: True if the security passes all Section 2.2 criteria (ESG rating, Controversies Score, all CBI screens, and unrated treatment); used in event-driven addition checks (Section 3.3) and quarterly addition checks (Section 3.2).
mock_datapoints.source_section: 3.3 Ongoing Event-Related Maintenance

mock_datapoints.name: cbi_screen_flag
mock_datapoints.description: True if the security is screened out by any of the controversial business involvement criteria in Section 2.2.3 and Appendix II; used in quarterly deletion check.
mock_datapoints.source_section: 3.1.1 Updating the Eligible Universe

final_asserts: All selected securities have msci_esg_rating in {AAA, AA, A, BBB, BB}
All non-constituent selected securities have msci_controversies_score >= 3
No selected security has any CBI flag triggered (producer flags, revenue thresholds)
Cumulative ff_market_cap coverage per gics_sector is between 0.45 and 1.0 for all sectors with at least one eligible security
Weights are all positive; weight sum across all selected securities equals 1.0 (or sums to 1.0 within each regional index)
No security absent from the Parent Index is selected

external_references.section: MSCI Global Industry Classification Standard (GICS) Methodology
MSCI Global Investable Market Indexes Methodology
MSCI Global ex Controversial Weapons Indexes Methodology

external_references.item: Listed in Appendix V Methodology Set; URL not provided in source

clarifications_needed.quote: For some securities, such data may not be published by MSCI Solutions by the end of the month preceding the Index Review. For such securities, MSCI will use ESG data published after the end of month, when available, for the rebalancing of the Indexes.
clarifications_needed.concern: The methodology does not specify a cutoff date for accepting late-published data (e.g., how many business days after month-end). It is unclear whether there is a hard deadline before the pro forma announcement or whether data published up to the announcement date is accepted.

clarifications_needed.quote: Fossil Fuel Extraction ... No specific restrictiveness level applied
clarifications_needed.concern: Fossil Fuel Extraction, Thermal Coal Power, Palm Oil, and Arctic Oil & Gas are described as having 'No specific restrictiveness level applied', unlike the other eight CBI categories which map to Most/Highly/Least Restrictive levels. It is unclear whether this means they are excluded from the four-level framework entirely, or whether additional operational guidance exists for how these screens are classified internally by MSCI Solutions.

clarifications_needed.quote: the market capitalization coverage of the sector to which the security belongs is less than 45%
clarifications_needed.concern: For intra-review event-driven additions (section 3.3), it is unclear whether the 45% sector coverage threshold is measured using current market prices (post-drift since last review) or using the end-of-month data used for the most recent review. The quarterly review text (section 3.2) implies current market cap is used (price movements cause deviations), but the event addition rule does not specify.

Appendix II names four restrictiveness levels (Most, Highly, Moderately, Least) but no CBI screen in this methodology is assigned to 'Moderately Restrictive'. It is unclear whether the level is reserved for screens applied by other MSCI ESG indexes or is simply unused here.

scoped_out.quote: The MSCI Selection Indexes ('the Indexes') are free float-adjusted market capitalization-weighted indexes designed to represent the performance of companies that are selected from an underlying index based on Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) criteria.
scoped_out.concern: Section 1 (Introduction) provides index overview and context only; no transformation rules are defined. All substantive rules are captured in Sections 2 and 3 and the Appendices.

scoped_out.quote: The following regional Indexes are aggregated to construct the MSCI World Selection Index. ... The MSCI World Selection Index and the MSCI EM Selection Index are aggregated to construct the MSCI ACWI Selection Index. ... The MSCI USA IMI Selection Index is created by aggregating the MSCI USA Selection Index and the MSCI USA Small Cap Selection Index.
scoped_out.concern: Section 2.3 describes the structural aggregation hierarchy (regional -> World/EM -> ACWI; USA + USA Small Cap -> USA IMI; ACWI subsets -> EAFE/China/etc.). These are index-aggregation rules, not per-security transformations. The construction-level distinction is captured in selection.content.constructed_at.

scoped_out.quote: The Large Cap and Mid Cap size-segments of the Indexes are derived from the Standard size-segment.
scoped_out.concern: Section 2.3 describes derivation of size-segment subsets from the Standard Selection Index. No new selection or weighting transformation; size-segment partitioning is an aggregation operation outside the scope of this methbook.

scoped_out.quote: MSCI ESG Ratings aim to measure entities' management of environmental, social and governance risks and opportunities. MSCI ESG Ratings use a weighted average key issue calculation that is normalized by industry to arrive at an industry-adjusted ESG score (0-10)...
scoped_out.concern: Section 4 (MSCI Solutions) describes external data products used as inputs; no index construction transformations are defined here. The data products are captured in data_requirements.mandatory_products.

scoped_out.quote: Prior to the May 2013 Index Review, the MSCI Selection Indexes were constructed using Global Socrates ESG Ratings.
scoped_out.concern: Appendix III (Methodology Transition) documents historical methodology changes. The methbook captures the current live rules only; historical thresholds and transition events are not modeled.

scoped_out.quote: The MSCI Country and Region Selection Indexes are constructed by applying the index construction rules detailed in Section 2 at the level of the respective country or region covered by the index, instead of the specific regional level used in the construction of MSCI Selection Indexes.
scoped_out.concern: Appendix IV (MSCI Country and Region Selection Indexes) describes a variant application of the same Section 2 and 3 rules at a different geographic granularity. No new transformations are defined; the variant follows the same rules and review cycle.

scoped_out.quote: The following sections have been modified as of November 2014...
scoped_out.concern: Appendix VI (Changes to this Document) is a document changelog. No transformation rules are defined.
